title: terminalia gracilis var. gracilis
intro: Terminalia gracilis var. gracilis, also known as the Forest Kola or White Kola, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Combretaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Terminalia gracilis var. gracilis include reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The herbal preparations of this plant include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used in various forms, including capsules, tablets, and topical creams.
